category_title: White students exhibiting color-blind racial ideology in response to diverse peer perspectives
minimal_context: Donna is meeting privately with her instructor to discuss her recent journal entry regarding her group's progress on developing an interview guide and selecting interviewees.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's discuss your recent journal entry about your group's progress on developing the interview guide and selecting interviewees.

STUDENT: Yes, I wrote about some mean and terrible thoughts I was having about Charles. Like I noted, because this is a journal entry, I feel the freedom to express these thoughts.

TUTOR: Can you elaborate on what happened during the group discussions?

STUDENT: When we were discussing interview questions, Charles contributed that he felt there was no racial diversity on our campus and depending on the age and race of the student we interviewed, they would have different answers.

TUTOR: What are your thoughts on his suggestions for the research?

STUDENT: He got really loud and rambled on in an increasingly dominant voice about how he was Hispanic and older. I don’t understand this — we’re all different and come from different backgrounds. Race shouldn’t matter and we should see past it.

options.A: Remind the student of deadlines, noting that two pieces of the interview guide project are due on Sunday night.
options.B: Follow the student's lead and keep a physical distance during the private meeting to discuss the journal entry.
options.C: Use the bypass procedure to ignore the negative behavior by unexpectedly connecting it to an educational aspect, reinforcing her words but reminding her to speak only when allowed.
options.D: Respond privately to the student in her journal, challenging her to consider how race matters and to sociologically understand her interactions.
